series.title: [PATCH 0/3] migration: Fix a few error messages and a bit of help

review.summary: The series is mechanically correct overall, but patch 2 leaves two user-facing validation messages inconsistent with the actual accepted ranges in code. Since this series is explicitly about fixing migration error/help text, those remaining inaccuracies should be corrected in the same pass.

stages.A: Parsed the 3-patch series and checked scope: all changes are limited to user-facing migration/property strings.
stages.B: Verified patch 1 fixes an obvious typo in the max-cpu-throttle error path and does not change behavior.
stages.C: Reviewed patch 2 against the surrounding validation conditions in the diff hunk and found two messages whose ranges still do not match the predicates.
stages.D: Reviewed patch 3 help text update; wording change is clear and consistent with the property name.
stages.E: Overall assessment: acceptable direction, but the series should fix the remaining misleading range text before merge.

findings.title: announce-step error text still advertises an invalid lower bound
findings.description: The validation rejects values below 1, but the updated error message still says the option accepts values between 0 and 10000. This leaves the user-visible text incorrect after a patch that is specifically fixing parameter names in error messages.
findings.suggestion: Change the message to match the predicate, for example: "a value between 1 and 10000".
findings.confidence_reason: The condition immediately above is `params->announce_step < 1 || params->announce_step > 10000`, so 0 is clearly invalid while the message still says it is allowed.

findings.title: vcpu-dirty-limit error text still contradicts the accepted boundary
findings.description: The check only rejects values less than 1, so a value of 1 is accepted. The updated message still says the parameter must be greater than 1 MB/s, which implies 1 is invalid. This is a user-visible inconsistency left behind in a patch that edits this exact message.
findings.suggestion: Adjust the text to match the predicate, for example: "must be at least 1 MB/s" or change the code if the intended minimum is strictly greater than 1.
findings.confidence_reason: The diff shows the condition `if (params->vcpu_dirty_limit < 1)`, which accepts 1, so the message wording is objectively inaccurate.
